Site Documentation
Site Dossier
Site Overview

Site--1 is a permanent Foundation installation at extrasolar distance. It observes anomalous phenomena at interstellar range and relays communications between Foundation deep-space assets.

The facility sustains a localised gravitational distortion field produced by an anomalous object housed in the lower structure. The object is not described in this document. The field is stable under normal operating conditions and is what makes both the observation and communication functions possible.

From any external vantage point, the facility is not visible. The gravitational distortion field it generates produces an appearance indistinguishable from a stellar-mass black hole at range. This is expected. It is not one.

Site--1 holds no SCP objects. It is not a containment facility. Personnel assigned here are not containment staff.
Access to this dossier requires Level 4/EHO clearance. Certain subsections require Level 5/GC. If you are reading this without appropriate clearance, contact Security Division immediately.
Physical Description

Site--1 is a vertical structure approximately 500 stories in height. Construction materials are classified. Its gravitational signature has been independently detected by non-Foundation instruments on two occasions. Disinformation protocols are active for both.

Floors 01 — 120
Lower Structure
Engineering, power systems, and the gravity core. Core at Floor 12. Science Division monitoring at Floor 15 — instrument read-only. No chamber access below Level 5/GC.
● Level 5/GC Required
Floors 121 — 340
Mid Structure
Primary operational zone. Observation suites, relay hardware, signal processing, departmental offices. Most personnel spend their entire posting here.
● Level 2+ Required
Floors 341 — 500
Upper Structure
Administration, residential quarters, medical facilities. Emergency command at Floor 480. Floors 490–500 are designated evacuation staging areas.
● General Access
Lattice Ring System

Three concentric rings encircle the midstructure. Each performs a discrete function and cannot substitute for the others. The exterior of Site--1 is not survivable without active ring support.

RingDesignationFunctionPrimary Failure EffectStatus
Ring OneDeflectionMaintains structural boundary of the distortion field. Prevents uncontrolled extension. Primary shielding against microparticle impact and radiation.Field boundary loss. Exterior lethality threshold breach at high severity.Nominal
Ring TwoStabilityRegulates field coherence. Suppresses fluctuation events. Issues tend to present before they are detected.Signal degradation. Observation data integrity loss.Nominal
Ring ThreeRelayPrimary antenna array. Interfaces with the gravity core's distortion field to achieve effective transmission range. Why this interface works is not fully understood.Complete communication blackout. FST-3 risk elevated during outage.Nominal
Subsystem Emblems
Lattice Ring
Stabilization rings in gravitational shear zones.
Gravity Core
Generates and regulates the distortion field.
Relay Array
Deep-space signal transmission.
Quasar Backup
Failsafe. Engages on catastrophic primary failure.
Obs. Frame
Defines the observational window. Synchronizes temporal frames.
Delayed Link
Delayed universe observation channel. Temporal offset: Variable.
Inv. Shield
Protects against signal inversion and ontological feedback loops.
Communication Systems
Primary System
Gravitational Relay
Signals routed through the distortion field achieve effective range and coherence at this distance. All standard communication passes through this system. Personnel do not need to understand why this works in order to operate it, and most do not.
Effective Range: DATA EXPUNGED
Backup — Emergency Only
Quasar Relay
Uses designated quasar QR-7 as carrier medium. Slow. Inexact. Real-time communication not achievable. Encoding artifacts are a known limitation. A message arriving via this system should be treated as confirmation that something has gone wrong.
Uses to date: ██ — higher than projected
Operational Purpose

Site--1 was commissioned after a formal review found that the Foundation's existing infrastructure could not identify or respond to interstellar-range anomalous events before those events progressed. The review cited specific incidents. Those records are available at O5 clearance.

The facility monitors a defined region of space for phenomena consistent with known SCP profiles. It does not interact with what it observes. Observation data is compiled, relayed to relevant Foundation departments, and acted on elsewhere. Several classified deep-space operations also use Site--1 as a relay node. Their details are not covered here.

Assigned Departments
MTF Tau-19
"Long Ear" / CMD
The only MTF assigned to Site--1. Handles external threat response, perimeter security, and evacuation coordination. External reinforcement following a major incident would not arrive in time to be useful. Tau-19 operates accordingly.
Standing Complement: ██
Research
Dept / RSD
Processes and catalogues observation data. Provides analytical support to Foundation departments active in the monitored region. Research staff do not interact with the lower structure. This is by design.
Science
Division / SCD
Maintains observation and communication systems. Conducts ongoing study of the distortion field at the operational level. Runs the only non-restricted monitoring position near the gravity core — output stations at Floor 15. Floor 12 remains Level 5/GC restricted.
Medical
Dept / MED
Provides standard and emergency medical services. Maintains a standing monitoring program for physiological effects related to extended field exposure. Findings to date are inconclusive. Several have been logged and are not discussed in this document. The program continues.
Security
Division / SEC
Internal access control and personnel vetting. Coordinates with Tau-19 for external incidents. The most consistent enforcement task at Site--1 is lower structure access restriction. It requires more enforcement than anticipated.

Anomalies & Incidents
Observed Anomalies

Two anomalies have been logged and neither has been escalated. They are documented here because the decision was made not to omit them.

LOG-S1-A01 / SIGNAL ANOMALY
Unresolved
Signal Arrival Discrepancy
On ██ occasions, signals transmitted by other Foundation assets have arrived at Site--1 before their logged transmission timestamp. Discrepancies range from under two seconds to just over eleven minutes. Data integrity has not been affected in any recorded instance. Timestamp synchronisation error across distance is the working explanation. The eleven-minute instance does not fit that model. No alternative has been formally proposed.
LOG-S1-A02 / TEMPORAL ANOMALY
Unresolved
Temporal Drift Reports
A number of personnel — primarily those with longer postings in the mid and lower structure — have submitted reports describing subjective time experiences inconsistent with elapsed clock time. Both directions. Medical has not identified a cause. The reports have not been dismissed, which is a different thing from being investigated.
Incident Addenda

At approximately 0340 hours, the Relay ring entered an unplanned inward transmission state, directing output into the distortion field rather than outward. Communication blackout lasted ██ hours and ██ minutes.

On restoration, Science Division identified anomalous data present in Site--1's internal systems that had not been there prior to the event. The data was purged manually. Its origin was not determined. The content of the data prior to purging is DATA EXPUNGED.

This type of event has occurred ██ times since Site--1 became operational. The O5 Council is notified following each occurrence. No pattern has been formally identified.

Following an unplanned distortion field fluctuation, three personnel on assignment to the lower structure reported that they had been present at their stations for approximately four hours. Elapsed time by facility clock was eleven hours and ██ minutes. Medical found no physiological abnormalities. Reported subjective experience was consistent across all three accounts.

The fluctuation event itself lasted ██ minutes by facility clock. Personnel accounts of the event's duration averaged ██ seconds.

The ████████ personnel involved were transferred off-site within ██ days of the incident. No protocol revision was issued. The site director's recommendation to issue one was not approved.

Emergency Reference
Failure States

Three failure states are classified for Site--1. Full protocols are in ERM-1, distributed to all department heads and MTF Tau-19 command on arrival. Personnel should not wait for automated triggers.

FST-1
Ring Failure
Degradation or structural failure of one or more Lattice Rings. Consequences scale with which ring is affected. Full three-ring failure constitutes a site-loss event and triggers automated evacuation.
▲ Staged Response Protocol
FST-2
Gravity Instability
Unplanned fluctuation of the distortion field beyond operational parameters. Lower severity produces signal incoherence. Upper severity produces localised environmental effects in the lower structure. What occurs in the core chamber during high-severity FST-2 events is not described in this document.
▲ Immediate Evacuation Below Floor 120
FST-3
Relay Inversion
Failure mode in which the Relay ring transmits inward into the distortion field rather than outward. Results in complete communication blackout. Has previously introduced anomalous data into internal systems. The origin of that data has not been confirmed as external.
▲ O5 Notification Required Post-Resolution
Foundation Standard Issue / Ver. 1.0.0
Emblem Reference
Authorized use only. Misuse of Site--1 identifiers is punishable by Foundation Protocol S12.5. For classified archive usage, O5 clearance and above required.
Restricted
Origin Note

The gravity core was recovered during a separate classified operation and assessed as capable of sustaining the required distortion field under controlled conditions. Site--1 was designed and constructed around it.

Its nature, origin, and classification status are held in a restricted document maintained by the Office of Extrasolar Affairs. Requests for access are processed. Most are declined.

We Observe Beyond. So Humanity Endures.  •  Site--1  •  Event Horizon Observatory
